idea.title: Polish Pass
idea.hook: Spend a fixed budget of polish points on a 90%-done game to push it across the ship line before backers lose patience.
idea.prototype: Top-down view of a game project shown as a 6x4 grid of feature tiles (bugs, UI, audio, balance, vfx, perf). Each tile has a quality bar 0-100. You have 30 polish points to allocate across tiles in 1/3/5 chunks; each chunk has diminishing returns. After spending, the project ships and scores against a hidden threshold per tile category. Controls: tap tile to add chunk, right-click / long-press to refund.
idea.why_novel: Compresses the indie-dev 'last-mile polish' experience into a 60-second decision: every player has felt the trade-off of fixing the final bugs vs polishing the UI, but no puzzle game has framed it as a fixed-budget tile grid with diminishing returns AND a hidden per-category floor that punishes ignored axes. The meta-theme is fresh for the game-development audience itself.
idea.non_timer_pressure: Fixed budget of 30 polish points + diminishing returns per tile (POLISH_BUDGET=30, DIMINISH_CURVE=[1.0,0.6,0.35,0.2,0.1])
idea.lose_condition: Any feature category finishes below 40 quality after all 30 polish points are spent (gs=GAMEOVER when polishBudget<=0 AND minCategoryQuality<40)

sources.title: the polish-pass last week of dev
sources.snippet: Final-week polish decisions ('which bug to fix, which feature to drop') are the most stressful part of solo dev — almost no games have made that pressure the core mechanic itself.

sources.title: diminishing returns on overpolishing
sources.snippet: Devs keep over-polishing one shiny feature while neglecting baseline categories — backers notice the gap, not the polish. The math of marginal returns vs floor thresholds is real.

synthesizer_brief: Frame the indie-dev last-mile polish dilemma as a 60-second budget-allocation puzzle: 30 points, 6 categories x 4 tiles, diminishing returns on stacking, hidden 40 floor per category, ship at avg>=60. The pressure is the budget + the floor — no timer needed. Daily seed for variety.

alternatives.title: Agent Dispatcher
alternatives.hook: Route incoming tickets to specialist agents under fixed capacity
alternatives.why_novel: Multi-agent routing under capacity constraints, language-neutral

alternatives.title: Ice Shatter
alternatives.hook: Crack an ice sheet with precise hit placement before it spans the screen
alternatives.why_novel: Spatial crack-propagation with optimal-cut puzzle layer

alternatives.title: Civ Pivot
alternatives.hook: Pivot a civilisation's tech tree mid-crisis under one-shot resource budgets
alternatives.why_novel: One-shot branching decisions instead of long sims

alternatives.title: Overcharge Donut
alternatives.hook: Balance an overheating reactor by routing power loops through donut-shaped coils
alternatives.why_novel: Closed-loop topology constraint with thermal feedback
